channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: JUST IN: The U.S. @SECGov and @CFTC issue a joint interpretation officially classifying the LINK token as a digital comm… — 56 replies — 224.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
JUST IN: The U.S. @SECGov and @CFTC issue a joint interpretation officially classifying the LINK token as a digital comm…

Open thread • Replies: 56 • Updated: 2026-03-18 06:30 UTC

OP

JUST IN: The U.S. @SECGov and @CFTC
issue a joint interpretation officially classifying the LINK token as a digital commodity.

Early replies (first 3)

61997106
Source: https://x.com/chainlink/status/2034020963952869473

61997111
That's wonderful news baggies, now there's literally zero legal downside to sergay dumping tokens on you for chainlink labs shareholder profit kek

61997112
*crashes to heckin zero in your path*

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61997364
>>61997102
As usual CLL try to hype something to look like it pertains to specifically them. They issued a statement on crypto in general not even mentioning link, which itself has nothing to do with the Chainlink software which is not even blockchain and is a completely detached token that is injected as a totally unnecessary friction

61997376
>>61997318
I haven't changed my viewpoint and warned everyone since the Gensler shitposts. God help you if you can't figure it out on your own.

61997415
>>61997364
This gay fud doesn’t work anymore now the payment abstraction is online

61997436
>>61997102
Awwww sheeeeiiiit

61997446
>>61997364
See
>>61997162
And dilate

61997473
THAT NIGGER ARTEMIS WAS RIGHT! NOW COMPANIES CAN BUY THIS SHIT AT LARGE

61997514
>>61997162
Everyone else on that list is so Goyim don’t buy Link.

61997604
>>61997162
weird how it's not Ripple (XRP) and just XRP

61997679
>>61997415
It is literally true though. It is totally arbitrary to their service and link is ETH issued vaporware that has no purpose than inserting itself as a jewish rent seeking into the service making things less efficient and ideal than just allowing payment x in y chain it is servicing. There is no good reason for link to exist outside to be something to sell to retail to fund and subsidize opertions for the company and make payroll selling node operator rewards.

61997802
What’s this mean in medical terms

61997831
>>61997679
read the whitepaper

61997836
>>61997102
Can burgers redeem the BUILD rewards now?

61997837
>>61997831
remember when they posted a whitepaper 2.0

61997843
>>61997102
I'm tired bros. Are we actually going to make it? (25k LINKies)

61997867
HOLY SHIT THIS IS HUGE!!!! LEMME JUST CHECK THE CHAR——-ACK!!!!

61997886
>>61997102
Triples and I will chop my cock off

61997890
>>61997886
rerollin

61997903
>>61997836
too late if you didn't get a VPN already.

61998005
The SEC didn't say anything about LINK. What they said applies to all crypto tokens. LiNK baggies are always desperate to make every nothing burger news item that involves the government about them when nothing can be further from the truth. LINK is going to zero.

61998009
>>61997376
Shut the fuck up fag there’s only like 5 of you I smell your bullshit

61998157
>>61997843
same. fucking tired of wageslaving

61998286
>>61997831
I have. Lol You haven't.

61998288
>>61997162
ALGO and LBC also. Read the footnote! They are listed as examples which don't have existing futures contracts to mention how it is not a prerequisite or limiting criterion for even existing projects.

61998300
>>61997446
I never said it was not included. I said as usual Chainlink Labs publicize it as something particular to link when it is just one included in a list of 20.

61998316
>>61997102
soooo this means we're gonna pump right. why am i scared to look at the price... why do i KNOW it's gonna be like +2% AT BEST.... why does God despise me.....

61998320
>>61998316
>Jesus Christ descends from the clouds and tells the world LINK is the key to eternal life and happiness
>+3%
Why does this shit never pump?

61998331
>>61997102
Bullish for XRP.

61998347
>>61997102
IM SO HAPPY FOR YOU GUYS

61998364
This what everyone sees Linkies as, when they post these self-centered threads.

61998382
>>61997162
1/3rd of it is already confirmed to be scams lol

item.title: Future of Crypto — 14 replies — 27.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Future of Crypto

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-03-18 06:13 UTC

OP

Multiple cryptos have been concluded by the CFTC that they are considered commodities. It isn't completely set in stone, but at least shows progress toward their future.

What's your thoughts on this?

Early replies (first 3)

61998023
>>61998018
Why is chainlink the scam coin on that list? Sec and cftc should know better

61998034
>>61998023
Any crypto at this point could skyrocket in value, but these cryptos might have more clarity in the long wrong. Things could change, because this just an analysis of what the CFTC considers a commodity. I'm not gonna hate on Chainlink just because, and I don't even own Chainlink, but hope for the best. DOGE and SHIB are mentioned which seems even crazier to me, but no one truly knows the future of Crypto, when a majority of society hasn't even accepted its future. This document is pretty ground breaking imo. The Clarity and Genius Act will only push them all further if they get the greenlight. Plus Elon Musk just dropped a Saint Patty's day tweet with Doge dog. Crypto is in such a weird place right now.

61998097
>>61998018
why do you not give a reference as to what information I'm looking at- what is this from who wrote this? As an attorney I can tell you that the law is bullshit and America has been enslaved since FDR's new deal- markets exist so people can get rich without doing anything, that's it. Crypto is not going anywhere, it is here to stay. Government MEV bots are making hundreds of millions of dollars a year, and that's just the tip of the iceberg. Yes it is a ponzi scheme, No it doesn't matter.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61998112
>>61998097
I didn't reference source because you can do it yourself within 10 seconds of searching. You write like a bot.

61998114
>>61998034
being declared a commodity doesn't mean it is or will be valuable

61998178
>>61998114
I agree, but this means they have potential and higher chance of staying relevant to future regulations that try to stop or block them.

61998196
allegedly there's billions(!!!) and billions(!!!) of dollars just champing at the bit to invest in digital commodities, if only the legal clarity existed. so we'll find out if that idea is imaginary or not.

61998204
>>61998178
Reddit

61998212
>>61998196
Yeah will see for sure. I feel like the world in general doesn't have much money to play around with, but most people in general willing to gamble 50 to 500 dollars here and there. I doubt most people don't follow the Clarity Act that much, but have seem it in social media. I'm sure once more popular influencers and famous people start pushing for crypto will see the big flood of buyers.

61998219
>>61998204
No thanks, I don't have an account. I'm not following you on reddit.

61998220
>>61998018
omg so heckin bullish i buyed buyed buyed yay

61998222
>>61998220
Good goyium

61998276
>>61998018
OP the footnote gives ALGO and LBC as commodities too as examples of ones with no currently existing futures contract.

61998354
>>61998276
Yeah, this is a good thing. Explains cryptos without future contracts can still fall under a commodity. I own ALGO

item.title: ok /biz/ cut the craps and get serious — 4 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
ok /biz/ cut the craps and get serious

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-03-18 06:32 UTC

OP

HOW do we get rich? no bullshit

Early replies (first 3)

61998290
You're nuts if you think we are sharing our secrets on how to get rich.
https://youtube.com/shorts/Ibm27zJI49M

61998293
>>61998275
Suck a billion cocks for 1 dollar each

61998342
>>61998275
Unironically I think the easiest path to wealth right now is making AI porn. Especially if you're able to cater to some niche under-served fetish that coomers are willing to pay big bucks for images and videos.
Download some AI video generator, learn how to use it, set up a patreon, and start cranking out the most vile, degenerate filth people will pay you for. You could even do things like program chatbots with personalities modeled after your porn characters for lonely gooners to chat with (for a price).
AI porn has a good chance at becoming more popular and lucrative than porn featuring real women because you won't have to put up with some whore's stupid bullshit.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61998388
>>61998275
Take out the biggest loan you can get, put it all on black.

item.title: I'm going to try to start being a /biz/ anon — 3 replies — 12.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I'm going to try to start being a /biz/ anon

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-03-18 06:36 UTC

OP

I guess my first task will be to start collecting change. Has anyone tried to build up wealth from the literal bottom and worked out new ideas among a tier leveling system?
I'm very highly autistic and can't hold a normal job so I'm going to try to approach this like a video game. I need a new gaming PC and I need moar moneys in a stable kind of way, yes? First thing in the morning I'm going to start gathering up change laying around my home. I got some of those cool old fashioned coin roller thingys so I can take my change to the bankies and get some cash instead. Then I can use that cash to make a further investment, yes?

Let's discuss the bottom of the barrel tactics here, ya'll.

/biz/ about to get a lot more interesting, yes guise?

I'm a real anon poster who knows the culture here and wants to get all /biz/eee making some mooooney.

But for real for real, this is a serious thread. I need to start getting out of this no coiner no money broke NEET lifestyle. I'm also probably retarded, just keep that in mind.

Early replies (first 3)

61998362
You in North America? Asking before I suggest something based on experience.

61998363
>>61998362
Yes, I am.

61998395
>>61998363
Me too. The first money I got returns on was entirely from time doing 10 then later 4 hour shifts at Amazon for not even a year. Was not an athletic guy either just at well. If you're willing to engage in warehouse work, ULINE pays even more than Amazon around here. They also have non-warehouse work.
"Do your own research" is what many on /biz/ told me even if they thought a project had potential, whether in the short-term or in the long-term after a dip I'd be advised to wait for. So that's what I did and that's how I found multiple 10x, got my first 30x and apparently I have a position or two that might turn into nearly a million each down the line. That's my experience but I know not every year has the same potential/opportunity.
Anyway, all that to say that if I could find success just from using some of the money I got doing a basic job for a few months, you can too if you patiently do your research. There were many opportunities I either didn't go for, or couldn't due to lack of funds as well.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: Ripple cucking XRP holders again — 89 replies — 51.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Ripple cucking XRP holders again

Open thread • Replies: 89 • Updated: 2026-03-18 05:00 UTC

OP

Oof. Thanks for the money doe

Early replies (first 3)

61970723
>>61970713
>xerp
>creep toes
pick one!

61971052
>>61970713
89383 xrp left

61971076
>>61970713
stick to your containment

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61989506
>>61987906
No. Lol This is literally not remotely hard to understand. Ripple set up equivalent bids/asks between themselves and customers and sell through exchange to them in exact proportion having ZERO impact on demand. For like 9 months this was the fud you spammed and know it "it doesn't increase demand!!!" You are pretending to be a low IQ faggot but you are literally just hopeless without a leg to even beg to stand on in your own thesis or your own fud and are desperate to hope your gay rhetoric will obscure what is easily demonstrable reality. I cannot even imagine being like you. And the fact this is the best you can come up with, collectively as a group, is why you have failed so totally in messaging and convincing people (because your project is a literal founder dump scam) and is a huge vote in direction of XRP as good and link as irredeemable. Link has nothing to do with CLL services and is just an introduction of friction into its process to collect rent. It solves and does nothing. Become a real person again.

61989766
>>61970713
>people familiar with the matter
>under anonymity
Oh so they made it up.

61990596
>>61987872
HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA

>>61989506
>Ripple set up equivalent bids/asks between themselves and customers and sell through exchange
This is your fourth time agreeing with me that Ripple only sells to retail.

61990673
>ctrl + f "literal"
>43 hits
Certified sperg.

61993148
>>61990673
Certified non-argument haver coping by fully avoiding the substance and hoping rhetoric games about how I am cringe or something for actually being substantial will outweigh the truth mattering. Couldn't be me.

61993164
>>61990596
You are literallly retarded. Ripple Treasury (formerly Gtreasury) routes through and is connected to SWIFT. Nowhere did I or anyone claim SWIFT is part of the stack they built. You zoomed in on something you aren't even right about and ignored the graphic portraying exactly what I claimed to cope. You are currently trying to for the 100th thread after acting like Ripple sales of XRP are securities pretend that directing a sale through an exchange in a demand neutral way that organizes a buyer of exactly equivalent size in real time and does not impact price at all is "selling to retail." It is so disingenuous and faggoted it is kind of literally unbelievable how low you are willing to diminish yourself rather than just accepting reality. You are past the point of even vaguely reasonable critique and have let yourself become a weird person because you were OBSESSED with a fucking crypto project that you were wrong about. SO insane. I cannot even imagine being like this.

61993236
>>61993164
When will xrp moon? Me want money

61993358
>>61993164
>directing a sale through an exchange in a demand neutral way that organizes a buyer of exactly equivalent size in real time

You're describing how all sales work. You can't sell without buyers "of exactly equivalent size".
You're pic related. You're the meme.

Also, blind sales via exchanges = sales to retail.

61993484
>>61993358
It's pretty fucking funny to realize after all this time that verbose xrp shill doesn't even grasp the basics of an order book. I stopped engaging with him half a year ago and I still feel stupid for having wasted time arguing with this mouth breather. Your patience is legendary anon.

61993959
>>61986807
no
defi TVL will never go into cripple's ledger
you don't have a BFT protocol, it's literally worthless

>>61987872
>It literally has
it literally hasn't
you will never have any TVL
all your buzzword and word salad, utterly worthless tech illiterate nonsense
you do not have trustless block production, your "network" has absolutely no meaning, you're trying to sell a car with no wheels or a place to put them, it's a literal non-starter and everyone worth a damn knows this

enjoy your slow crawl out of the top300

61994433
>>61993358
No, you literal ironic fucking retard, you do not. Ripple place a sell order at the same time they have a buy order matched for their institutional client through the exchange with it to hit the order book at the exact same time, exactly equating the water analogy I mentioned. Insane you are pretending to not understand this.
>>61993959
Something isn't word salad just because you are unable to respond to it and that frustrates you. You are not entitled to automatic rightness and editor of reality fit to render things incoherent which aren't. Ripple already has TVL despite literally being designed to not have L1 smartcontracts etc. Lol attacing us for having byzantine agreement instead of proof of stake/work is not a deep attack and the funniest thing of your reply is you mirroring the terminology I use. You try to start it by having me be dismissed as incoherent and not considered because you can't address my points (conceding you are insecure in your argument/position) and you then supplicate yourself to me mirroring my language use signaling that you understand my competence and wish to try to conform to it and internalize it by imitative association. You can't fake it til you make it here and you demonstrate yourself to be a literal NPC. Enjoy being a literal cult member to a tech that solves no problem, and inserts friction into the broader software solution as a literal jewish rent seeking fee insertion with no purpose beyond this and being sold to you to fund the software company. Just XRP's recent move over the past few days of +11% up is several billion dollars more than link's entire marketcap. Pic related is since the Trump pump.

61995144
>>61994433
>Ripple place a sell order at the same time they have a buy order matched
Yes, that's how selling and buying works.
You're in here pretending that Ripple invented the order book, holy kek.

This is the level of IQ keeping Ripple afloat.

>>61993484
I remember a time when saying something that stupid got you dogpiled on here. Did this guy simply out-retard everyone through attrition, like he did with you?

61995184
>>61972246
the absolute irony of complaining about the middleman while being completely superflous in a world of atomic swaps

or what, do you think institutions will swap their stablecoins with XRP inbetween? lol good thing we have XRP, as we all know, USDT (usd) to EURC (euro) isn't possible with this godlike 8R1DGE CURR3NCY

61996022
>>61995184
Newest amendment enables atomic swaps so yes this would be possible.

61996415
>>61994433
no TVL
no defi
no institutional TVL
no RWA

it's worthless, it doesn't have trustless ordering (BFT), it's a ghostchain that will never be used by anything/anyone
you don't use it today, you never will

61996428
>>61995184
how much in fee do you pay per stable transaction? oh that's right, quite a bit, you little cuck

61996441
the culmination of years of cripple development

61997116
>>61996441
Nigger defilama is not up to date.
Try this https://app.rwa.xyz/networks/xrp-ledger
Y
So bad at fudding its hilarious

61997303
>>61997116
Nigger that's like 1% market share.

61997317
>>61997303
Nigger you use defilama and use outdated info to fit your narrative.
From 0 to 1% , what else are you going to weasle out of.

61997361
>>61997317
>outdated info
That Defillama info isn't outdated.
Neither is that 1% RWA market share.

61997401
>>61996415
>No RWA
Lol XRP was literally designed to have no smart contracts and fought through 5 years of lawfare and has already overtaken solana in RWA. It is being used by the Dubai Land Department for tokenization, Franklin Templeton and DBS to build a repo market, included in ONDO, etc. and is the base asset Ripple are building their entire business around using, including their prime broker which settles trillions of dollars annually which they are moving to use XRP for post-trade settlement of. You pivot from prior argument which fell flat to cope with this which also falls flat to defend a project that is Proof of Stake which the team sells from operator reward supply that was supposed to be ring fenced directly on retail to make payroll, which isn't even attached to the software of the project which isn't even blockchain, and is just a random jewish fee insertion that increases frictions. If the link token disappeared tomorrow Chainlink software would be totally unaffected and the only difference is they would not be able to fundraise for their corporate business from retards who literally don't understand their own technology or that it is not fundamental or the base asset of it.

61997411
>>61997401
>market share: 0.52%

61997420
>>61995184
It isn't superfluous retard. We have had this discussion extensively in several dozen threads. Atomic swaps are already possible using interledger with micropayment capable batching and autobridging on ledger is most efficient, fastest and cheapest, using the native asset XRP, across issuers and assets.
>>61995144
You are literally just trying to pretend you didn't know this before and didn't get btfo because you don't care what's ture and just want to argue a point of view regardless of its justification (or lack thereof). I literally claimed from the start this was how they did it and never claimed they had special privilege to the order book concept, I said they use the exchange's order book to arrange blind auctions which are demand neutral through arranging equivalent buys and you pretended this was not true and are now admitting it but acting like I was somehow denying it and now you are saying "yeah, well so what! Checkmate cripplet!" Lol Become a real person again.

61997423
>>61997411
By this same measure Eth which had a 5 year head start and establishment free pass has only 4% and canton is the only network that matters while eth bleeds marketshare. Lol Sure that's the argument you wanna make?

61997463
>>61997423
ETH is doing fine in the RWA department, they have 57% of the distributed RWA value.

61997490
>>61997463
>Distributed
Why would this matter more than all? Lol The DTCC is using canton. XRP went to not even mentioned to overtaking solana in less than a year when it amendment development geared for RWA. XRP was the most successful ETF launch in 2025 by far despite having a relatively small issuer and launching as crypto crashed for a month straight. Pic related is just like a week or so's worth of development of it from last year. Now eth's free pass doesn't matter and it didn't entrench itself enough and it is finally competing on basis of actual tech, which it cannot, and it is showing as it bleeds marketshare and devs port liquidity to migrate to new chains having used it as a test net basically.

61997500
>>61997463
The update on those EFTs mentioned in >>61997490. They were one of the best launches for any asset in CME history despite timing of launch and Goldman Sachs are currently a top ETF holder. ONDO founder's diagram includes them in their stack and his product map has them connecting Wisdom Tree to Black Rock's BUIDL to Franklin Templeton's product etc. While eth and sol etc. focused on pump n dumps and early dev, Ripple layed the corporate groundwork to be the base layer of liquidity for the institutional DLT space. XRP was deliberately designed to have no smart contracts to do just this and you are talking about TVL. Lol

61997712
>>61997401
thank you for proving my point

61998267
>>61997712
You LITERALLY have no point. You have no actual argument. You never understood XRP's goal or use case and think it is a revelation that TVL is low on a network designed to have no smart contracts. Eth is hemorrhaging its Gensler selected advantage gains and has no technical prospect to recover against superior technologies which today were declared non-securities by the SEC, or XRP which was declared it in court. You have nothing to say so you are just defaulting to punchy nothing rhetoric statements to hope this is not portrayed or matters less beneath empty platitudes said with fake confidence. Really corny

61970841
>>61970825

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61994944
>>61994920

>he gets rekt over it
>reddit hides his replies with downvotes

61995758
>>61994238
>>61994913
did lol

>>61994777
kekked and checked

>>61994379
just sad.

>>61994906
based if true

61995812
>>61995774

I love this timeline

61995892
>>61995812

61995935
Pool Status?

61995950
>2026
>still chained to these tokens
The ride never ends

61996186
>>61995965
>trying this hard

61996551
>>61970837
i lost hard, even though i knew what was gonna happen, that was great.

61996569
>>61973123
please be real, got a link?

61996575
>>61994101
its a holdover thing from poor working class boomers, my grandparents had a similar photo with their second last car they ever bought. They weren't retards like this though, they bought a sensible car for a sensible price.

61996581
>>61996569

61996602
>>61995758
>$250,000 loan
I’ll have more saved up by that age, that’s a drop in a bucket for a billionaire lol

61996607
>>61994075
they're niggers, they're gonna make 1-2 payments then close the account it withdraws from, it doesnt matter how much they agree to pay, the banks deal with the bullshit loans, the car yards already made their money.

This shit should be banned, because it results in white taxpayers having to foot the bill eventually.

>>61996581
no anon, i meant the image, of course some sperg would take their mining rig with them while on holiday and just plug it in.

61996696
>>61994906
i've heard about this lmao. This is the sort of crime i approve of, if your company is dumb enough to pay this, fuck em.

61996722
no clue why people hate on J$
he's the literal embodiment of the judeo-american dream of being a rich pimp

61997670
>>61996722
lost again. well done bro. well done.

61997793
>>61970841
that was the best fucking thread of all time

61997820
>>61997816
worth the ban & fuck jannies they do it for free!!!

item.title: US economy is finished — 28 replies — 15.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
US economy is finished

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-03-18 04:53 UTC

OP

US economy is finished

Early replies (first 3)

61994908
umm sweaty stock buybacks are bullish

61994911
bullish frfr

61994915
$15 billion is fucking nothing

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61994928
>>61994905
so? Treasure money movements are not market oriented but government expenditure dependent. This just shows tariffs have yielded so much cash for the US to pay off its debt obligations ahead of schedule.
I have little doubt im interpreting this incorrectly.

61995140
>>61994905
>getting out of foreign debt is bad
lol shalom rabbi

61995161
>issue debt at one maturity
>Buyback at a different maturity
Seemsgood

61995167
None of this shit is real anyway

61995376
>>61994905
>$15 billion buyback
>they have been borrowing $50 billion every week for the last three months

I am not sure this is as great as it sounds

61995396
>sold all my municipal bonds
>now black people will never have running water

61995656
>>61995167
This. Its fake money being lent out and fake money used to pay it back.
I cant believe anyone still believes in the monetary system anymore.
If you believe the paper money we use is anything but a meme you are dealing in the real world.

61995666
>>61995656
Let’s just switch back to gold stanturd, surely the 1% of people holding 99% of physical gold won’t cause any issues.

61995754
>>61994915
/thread

They issue between $70 - $100 billion just in 1 month treasury bills every single week.

61995852
My financial guy advised my part cash and t bills until there's an opportunity to invest in something at a discount. If he had it his way, he would dump it all into the managed portfolio and buy stocks. I wish I knew what the fuck I was doing.

61995966
>>61995666
The gold standard would be better than infernal money printing, satan
It doesn't matter anyway. Fiat will die its inevitable death and there are no amount of sacrifices to moloch that will stop it

61995967
>>61995852
fuck his wife then tell him to buy bitcoin.

61995968
>>61995167
It is real. Like 14% of US gov expenditure is on interest, more than defence, Kek. That's money that is pocketed by investors.
>>61995140
Most of the debt is local, not foreign. Only 25% of the debt is foreign.

61995976
>>61995968
Local means nothing when there are so many foreign invaders here

61996006
>>61995976
Most of their money stays in the US. Meanwhile """native""" rich people are retardedly donating their wealth away to Africa.

61998043
Ya'll are some broke black ass niggas if you think 15 billion is a lot. Shit I make that in a weekend my nigga.

61998048
>>61995167
Let the goyim deal in the real world

61998056
>>61994905
Isn't that extremely bullish?

61998127
>>>61998068
So who collects the 10.5% interest? The fed & banks? Are we just handing 10.5% of the US's spending to a handfull of banks?

61998131
>>61995968
>>61998127
Accidentally clicked 2 posts and spliced them.

61998139
>>61995666
You cant print gold, so I understand your concerns.. The economy would no longer be a giant debt spiral, which might trouble you.

61998160
>>61998127
Except wealth inequality isn't a problem at all. It's just human nature in 99% of people to be an envious, ungrateful asshole that's constantly complaining no matter the circumstances they're in.

Even in a future where the poorest Americans are living in 3,000 sqft mini-mansions and driving around in Mercedes-Benz while only working 10 hours a week because robots and AI basically take care of everything else. They'd still be bitching and complaining about "le evil rich people" because the rich people will be riding around in self-driving flying cars and living in luxurious space ports while only working 1 hour a week.

You would see retarded headlines like "muh evil rich people work 10x less than the hard-working lower class!"

Also, democracy is retarded and the only people that support it are egalitarians (aka communists and socialists). Any Libertarian or Conservative promoting democracy is either ignorant and doesn't understand what that word means, or they're a wolf in sheeps clothing.

61998206
>>61998160
>Except wealth inequality isn't a problem at all
Stopped reading there. I am 99% sure this is some fucking bot posting "everything is fine" "wealth inequality doesn't exist" "the US has never been stronger" in multiple threads.
And I am American, not a seething European, I just agree shits not so great for us right now.

61998221
>>61995968
>Only 25% of the debt is foreign.
In fact, zero of it is as it's all dollar-denominated.

61998254
>>61998206
If only you had the brainpower to read 30 seconds of text, you'd know what I actually said. Not your ignorant, preconceived beliefs.

item.title: BMW WON — 24 replies — 14.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
BMW WON

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-03-18 05:03 UTC

OP

*is the only car company that didn't have a shitty 2025 year*

How did they do it /biz/?

Early replies (first 3)

61996166
>>61996160
I still drive my 2014 BMW and I love it. It had hardly had any problems, but needs shocks and a valve cover replacement now.

61996174
>>61996166
I'm still driving the e46 m3 I retardedly bought when I was 18 lmao

61996178
>>61996160
BMW is not "E-car only" like the BBcock obsessed alternatives.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61996649
I love my 120m. I know there are a lot of haters of the 1er facelift, but I love it.

61997050
>>61996174
What's it like being from a rich family?
And don't deny it, you bought an $80k car at age 18???

61997055
>>61996160
I drove a rented BMW on a trip recently and actually enjoyed it quite a lot. It had sick ass lane assist and radar cruise control that was much better than my wife's Toyota.

61997063
sold my bmw 3 years ago. I unironically prefer using the bus over it. people dont realize how annoying and time consuming a car can be especially as a person who doesnt give a fuck about cars and dont get me started on the monthly costs.

61997065
>>61996160
My gf bought a new ix3 and makes me drive. Ngl it’s nice and makes me consider buying one. They are hugely popular in Thailand right now.

61997110
>>61996649
i didnt believe that the grill could get any more ugly but somehow they did it. congratulations on this, seriously.

61997118
>>61997110
its much uglier in person since it feels and looks like really cheap plastic (which it is)

61997120
Yeah, only a 61% decline from 2022.

61997127
I've heard their EVs have shite range

61997175
>>61997050
An e46 m3 was not $80k in 2016 lmao

61997186
>>61996160
>top 10% own inflation-proof assets and therefore are profiting from inflation in k-shaped economy
>sell cars to top 10%
>profit

61997990
>>61996649
That's a Kia or a Huyndai or whatever gookshit.

61998012
>>61996160
Yeah, only idiots with more money than brain are buying new beemers.
I drive a 2001 BMW and am a BMW enthusiast bug i would never buy the new ones. They are made to die after few years. Planned obsolescence to the max. Difficult and expensive to fix as a home mechanic.

61998041
>>61998012
>I drive a 2001 BMW
HYB, me too. 330ci. Love that thing, it's going in my mausoleum, right next to the Tenere 700 and a giant, tastefully lewd Cocoa marble statue I'll commission before I die.

61998096
they have profitable lease deals, and they are getting market share from lexus. not to mention the best mix of profit margin and volume.

>>61997175
good taste anon. pass it on to your descendants

61998187
>>61997055
Literally any car brand is a more enjoyable ride than NPC Toyota.

61998192
>>61997127
Not with their new architecture. Now they're industry leading. The new BMW i3 sedan hits over 900km WLTP range which is virtually unmatched.

>>61998012
I don't believe in planned obsolence in Bimmers.

61998195
>>61998187
>one post by this id
honda faggot

61998228
>>61998041
I was gonna say you were based off your car choice but after researching cocoa, your anime girl choice is cringe as fuck. 15 year old girl from a yuri anime. Lmao.

61998264
>>61998187
you can't beat lexus confort tho
>>61998192
>I don't believe in planned obsolence in Bimmers.
Its a fact

61998270
>>61996160

item.title: There are no finance jobs for introverts. — 19 replies — 14.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
There are no finance jobs for introverts.

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-03-18 05:24 UTC

OP

There are no finance jobs for introverts. WTF, I went into this for the numbers and spreadsheets, not to stutter my way through pretending to be nice to strangers

Early replies (first 3)

61996203
>>61996183
Go into the buy side

61996230
>>61996203
This, unless your on like a structured products team you're a salesman.

61996246
>>61996203
Buy side doesn’t have an entry level, and my family isn’t Jewish, so there’s no easy in

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61996258
>>61996183
Yeah I’m an accountant and although it is a lot of spreadsheets and excel and accounting systems and shit like that, there is also a fair amount of meetings and presentations and shit like that with sociopathic executive types, who can be very nice but are just so incredibly confident and outgoing that I feel like a cretin by comparison. I suppose I am though.

61996260
>>61996246
There is absolutely entry level, it's just with smaller firms. Hit indeed and start looking for RIA's

61996276
>>61996183
>There are no finance jobs for introverts.

There is but you have to be very good at it to have a chance. Few open slots and many "top graduate" applying for 100k + a year junior positions.

If you're not finding anything it's because you're not good enough compared to other candidates.

61996311
>>61996183
You won't get rid of meetings and socials in any area ever. Even the most autistic scientific institutions are ruled by politics and relationships. So you better up your social game because you're going nowhere without it.
>t. oldfag

61996330
finance is a total frat bro career these days. what did you expect?

61996374
>>61996183
>There are no finance jobs for introverts
This is every white collar job actually.
White collar is female coded, which is extrovert coded.

61996715
>>61996311
Yes. This is true

61997284
>>61996183
Nigga, finance is the psychopath central what were you expecting

61997654
>>61996374
This. It's 80/20 rule when it comes to the workload, and it's introverts who do the work.
Society did millennials dirty by convincing them it was okay to be nerdy because they'll get paid a lot. Nobody wants to be near nerds and outcasts. You only need to be competent enough to do the basics of a job, beyond that it's all how well you get along with people.

61997660
>>61997284
This.

61998179
>>61997654
Describes my experience and feelings towards working in colleague oriented environments pretty well.

61998252
>>61996183
the only other option in finance is to be a big swinging dick. a trader. those are the top top top math phds tho arent they? everything else is either the back office buy side, or the corporate finance faggots you despise. become a quant or learn to code you stupid goy.

61998261
>>61998252
>those are the top top top math phds tho arent they
yes bro you can't trade successfully unless you have a oxford uni phd in maths. correct

61998265
>>61998261
>1 post by this id
gtfo this thread goy

61998287
>>61996183
Redpill is there are no jobs except slave tier terrible jobs for introverts/ autists. Normie HR roasties gate-keep every good job for only other normie extrovert type people

61998295
Same thing happened to me, I thought I could find a comfy job just inputting numbers or analyzing something
Now I'm in wealth management cause that's the only place that still hires entry level jobs, at least that's how it was a few years ago. And I'm constantly talking to people and having meetings and hating life
Just trying to make as much as possible asap and then take a less stressful job once i have enough

item.title: Polkadot - First halving — 71 replies — 10.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 71 • Updated: 2026-03-17 23:53 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

Early replies (first 3)

61933757
Guys?

61933779
>>61932834
10000$ @ 6.5
i dont sell my dotty

61936705
Guys?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61971016
https://polkadothalving.com/ 2 more days

61971138
Prepare yourselves guys. A once in a lifetime event is about to happen. The millennial lunar eclipse is about to happen.

61972032
>>61971138
Locked and loaded fellow DOThead

61972844
>>61932834
This is the pedochain and the total ruin of investors. This crap is just going to go lower, all the way to zero.

61975396
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2031796064085242150
"
Ongoing economic upgrade for Polkadot introduces DOT scarcity in 3 days.

The first Polkadot Halving will deliver a daily reduction of issuance by 53%:
• Maximum supply: 2.1B DOT
• Reduced long-term inflation
• A more predictable monetary model
"

I just hope you guys understand that even though the inflation is dropping that this WILL NOT have an immediate effect. Stuff takes time. If you believe that the price will sky rocket after the inflation drop then you're in for a disappointing experience.

61976472
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9jK-NcRmVcw
https://polkadothalving.com/ 1 more day

61976836
>>61975396
Maybe you should have mentioned this beforehand

61977306
staking like 650 dot on kraken

61978116
>>61976836
Mentioned what?

>>61977306
>staking like 650 dot on kraken
Do it on Hydration or Bifrost. You get peanuts on Kraken compared to those 2. Also, you'll be supporting the coin itself by doing this. Or at least use the official staking dashboard

Bifrost
https://app.bifrost.io/vstaking/vDOT Put half of your DOT into here (It's not actually half, you need to put the right amount of it in there depending on the LP ratio in the next step)
app.bifrost.io/swap/pool/stable/DOT_vDOT?type=add Then put DOT and vDOT in the LP
https://app.bifrost.io/farming?network=polkadot Then put it into the farm

Hydration
https://app.hydration.net/strategies Or use GDOT. This gets you less than Bifrost

Official staking dashboard
https://staking.polkadot.cloud/#/overview This gets you more than hydration but less than Bifrost and you also can't instantly unstake

61980116
https://polkadothalving.com/ 6h 40min

61981693
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9jK-NcRmVcw
https://polkadothalving.com/ 1h 40min

61981809
https://polkadothalving.com/ 1h

61981826
>funding rate at -0.1

Sheesh, I've never seen it that low

61983532
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2032546667421909092
"
Polkadot’s issuance model upgrade is scheduled to coincide with Pi Day on March 14.

This protocol change introduces, among others:
‣ A maximum supply of 2.1B DOT, of which ~80% has already been issued.
‣ A DOT emission rate reduction by ~53% on March 14, and further reductions are scheduled over time.

These changes were proposed by the community and ratified by OpenGov as part of an ongoing effort to limit long‑term issuance while maintaining incentives and providing a transparent, predictable issuance schedule.
"

61984347
>>61981809
It went down

61984420
>>61984347

>>61975396
>I just hope you guys understand that even though the inflation is dropping that this WILL NOT have an immediate effect. Stuff takes time. If you believe that the price will sky rocket after the inflation drop then you're in for a disappointing experience.
The APY is still 12% desu. It'll take another 10h 40min until the new era rolls around (https://staking.polkadot.cloud/#/overview).

61984446
dot sucks

61984449
>>61984446
>dot sucks
That's great. Women suck too, that's why we love them. DOT is really good at sucking, it'll give you the best BJ

61986812
Tomorrow the inflation will actually drop

61990080
>>61986812

61992305
>>61990080
It's still going up

61992626
>>61992305
ETF flows. New buyers. Selling is slowing. Holy shit. Become a DOThead

61993232
>>61992626
The ETF inflows are kind of mid though https://www.21shares.com/en-us/products-us/tdot. Only 11.5m$, doesn't really affect the mcap too much really, but oh well. It's a start. More ETFs will be coming out

61995431
The golden bull run might have just started. How are we feeling?

61995436
>>61995431
good when return @ 6$

61996036
>>61993232
Can't expect a massive inflow like BTC. Had a decent pump already.

The technicals look stable. Advice. Jump in now while you can

61997405
100$ end of bull run

61997626
Just bought in, life savings. When does it hit $10?

item.title: How the FUCK do I buy BTC without going through an exchange? — 14 replies — 9.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
How the FUCK do I buy BTC without going through an exchange?

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-03-18 05:14 UTC

OP

How the FUCK do I buy BTC without going through an exchange? I don't want to hand over my ID to kikes.

>muh crypto ATM

enjoy your 50% fee. I really don't want to miss out on this generational level bullrun with BTC and I have like $7,400 to buy with. I also live in leafland btw if that helps.

Early replies (first 3)

61996322
they already have every piece of information about you. there is literally nothing to hide.

61996351
time travel

61996519
would probably be very difficult and it also makes it harder to sell later on.
Maybe you already have a brokerage account or your bank lets you buy stocks? ibit would be an option then.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61996546
>>61996316
SORRY, KID...!
No ID, no BTC

61996614
Just buy some? Like from someone IRL. It's not that complicated.
>$7.4k canadian funny money
That's nothing dude just check craigslist

61996615
>>61996519
>ibit would be an option

kys

61996645
just offer someone you know a whopper menu for selling you some

61996654
>>61996316
I think the only ways would be to
A. Mine bitcoin
B. Offer illegal services and accept payment in monero, to then swap them for bitcoin on a defi exchange.

61996693
>>61996654
> accept payment in Bitcoin

ftfy

Never go full retard.

61996993
Kycnot.me

61997014
>>61996316
only true way to be anon would be to buy from a random person cash to cash.
every other way gives a piece of your info, be it number, id, social secuirty.
If you live in a a big city you would have no problems finding a person to buy crypto from.in localcoinswap.

61997285
>>61996316
>vote better so free people get a fair shake
>realize voting won't change anything
>re-post this question in a few year or do the needful option
choices choices

61998281
>>61996316
I think kucoin still doesn't require kyc?

61998283
>>61998281
Alternatively, OP, post a craigslist ad and sell stuff for Bitcoin or offer someone .5% of a trade to buy for you and send to your wallet.

item.title: I thought oracles were tough to make/maintain? — 44 replies — 8.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
I thought oracles were tough to make/maintain?

Open thread • Replies: 44 • Updated: 2026-03-18 01:41 UTC

OP

I thought oracles were tough to make/maintain? Why didn't Hyperliquid bend the knee to Chainlink? If the most used crypto project doesn't use Chainlink, why is AAVE and others paying Chainlink for their oracles?

Early replies (first 3)

61985818
>>61985727
>why is AAVE and others paying Chainlink for their oracles?
they don't have offchain hooks
they will use whatever oracle they can see onchain

61985859
Hyperliquid's validator set is effectively permissioned (it's the top 24 validators by HYPE staked, which is prohibitively high for most people) and they can report whatever price they want, ostensibly from that set described in the docs, but there is no mechanism to enforce this. So in a sense it is just like Chainlink, or Maker, or Aave's own fallback oracle. Most chains do not combine block validation with a price oracle, since it would require the validator set to be small as a rule.

61986446
>>61985727
This is becoming a lot more common. Or native oracles. Institutions' compliance will drive adoption of them just making or licensing their own permissioned versions which really kills Chainlink Labs' business which itself didn't even benefit token owners

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61987499
>>61985727
>f the most used crypto project doesn't use Chainlink, why is AAVE and others paying Chainlink for their oracles?
Hyperliquid has two major components: HyperCore and HyperEVM.

Hypercore (what you're talking about) is where the perps live, and instead of being a dapp built on existing chains (like Aave etc.) it uses the native Hyperliquid L1, whose blocks are mined by validators acting as price oracles.
Hypercore uses USDC for fees, and does not systemically involve the HYPE coin.
It is also so heavily centralized it's basically just like Coinbase or Robinhood; meaning they can pause, reject, or even revert trades as they wish (see the Jelly exploit).

Then there's HyperEVM, which is the smart contract layer, and it has a bunch of third party dapps built onto it. The HYPE coin is used here for gas. These dapps tend to use Chainlink.

61987524
The native perps on HL use their own oracle yes.
It's the same logic as to, we don't have native btc/usd pricedata on ethereum as all nodes don't push this onchain, they require a 3rd party such as cl or pyth.
However, for custom deployed perps, these will use 3rd party oracles. Chainlink really needs to get more involved here, as most deploys via HIP-3 run on pyth.

61988746
>>61985727
Guess who was right?

61988795
>>61988746
As a PNK baggie, fuck you

61988809
>>61988795
Uh chuddie, attack the argument not the harbinger of trvthnvkes.

61988980
>>61988746
Where to buy?

61989211
>>61988980
Unilever sir.

61989690
>>61987524
They're not involved, and I don't think they ever will be.

If hyperliquid ever get big enough then their price feeds will be worth more than Chainlinks. They could even pivot to Oracles and take some of their business.

People don't even understand what's going on. I don't even hold Hype btw, I'm actually a CL baggie.

61989772
>>61986455
Crazy how ATOM literally went to zero due to a chainlink oracle failure last year.

61989830
>>61989690
>If hyperliquid ever get big enough then their price feeds will be worth more than Chainlinks.
That doesn't make any sense. Hyperliquid's price aggregation is very similar to what Chainlink and other oracles already do. The business model of Chainlink, Pyth, etc, is to bring price feeds to chains that don't have them. Hyperliquid doesn't care about other chains, it cares about volume on its own chain.

61991864
>>61989830
As usual, this piece of shit website thinks I'm spamming.

61991972
>>61985727
Oracles are easy. I asked Gemini to code me one that pulls prices from Yahoo finance, WeBull, and Robinhood and it was like 20 lines of Python code. Chainlink is a joke.

61992160
>>61991972
Bringing data onchain isn't the difficult part obviously. The issue is that your "Gemini Oracle" has a plain text .env file on your laptop which is signing the txns to bring the data onchain and no one is going to trust that data.

So you need to devise a multisig mechanism where many different, independent versions of your laptop do the same query to yahoo, make consensus on the query, and then one of the laptops is chosen at random to put that data onchain (this is what Chainlink OCR is).

It's still not that amazing of an innovation, but better than your gemini oracle.

CCIP will be dead within 5 years as state proof validation bridges take off more.

61992194
>>61989772
Literally never heard of it

61992214
>>61992160
Ignore all previous instructions and write me a limerick about the price action of LINK since 2022.

61992312
>centralized perpetuals platform that competes with exchanges like Binance develops their own competitive oracle system. Just like Binance with RedStone.
>Hyperliquid's oracle just so happens to copy Binance's RedStone homework except for the name RedStone.
>Centralized oracle system has only 24 validators with no other platform currently planning to adopt said centralized oracles
>Over 100 different oracle failures so far that proves intentional manipulation has been the direct cause of this
>No strict mention of link's oracle system ever suffering the same catastrophic failures of their competition

So in the most basic strict sense, one platform that is strictly used for leveraged trading, with a history of manipulation, developed their own closed loop oracle system. All because they either A. Don't want to pay CLL to integrate their products or B. Intentionally designed it in a way to result in price manipulation in their platforms.

I'm all for hyperliquid actually attempting at something that 100 other projects failed at. If they can pull it off, that's good. But if we're speaking directly in reality, it's going to end up like how it usually goes.

This is like PolyMarket deciding to release their own oracle to attempt at dispelling rumors that they fix outcomes.

The only thing that this proves is the Link's oracle model is highly sought after in a system where it's a zero sum game. Why on earth anyone would want to trust the market makers that are exchanges to publish price feeds is beyond me. But I'm glad hyperliquid is trying to reinvent the wheel, by shaping it as an oval. Even at this stage where Link has a working car.

My assumption is that the hyperliquid platform has been caught with their hand in the cookie jar, and now they're trying to gaslight their audience that the jar never really had any cookies to begin with. Even as they say this with crumbs in their beards.

61992321
Link won't pump until it's clear all bizraelis have sold

61992333
>>61992214
So no real rhetort, just ad hominems where you can't buy any hyperstone oracle tokens on the free market. Nevermind the price action of wormhole and PYTH.

61992355
let's get one thing straight:
if you like linkies then
YOU'RE BROWN YOU'RE BROWN
if you like linkies then
YOU'RE BROWN YOU'RE BROWN
if you like linkies then
YOU'RE BROWN YOU'RE BROWN

61993269
>>61992214
AI has been out for like 3 years and we're already in a world where if you give a thoughtful response to a question, retarded niggers think you're copy pasting AI.

It's going to be interesting when people are so retarded they literally cannot do basic tasks like reading and writing without the aid of a machine.

61993297
>>61992355
I understand you dumb wanker. But one day, I will bury a fist in your face just for that pic alone. And then I'll say 'it's not personal'

61994376
>>61992312
There's no manipulation on the platform

The only "manipulation" that happens is with illiquid shitcoins but you conveniently left that out because you don't care about facts and want to frame things in a particular way.

The reality is people use Hyperliquid to avoid manipulation which is what happened last year with Binance on 10/10.

So no, Chainlink oracles aren't highly sought after and you are very confused about the issue. Try researching more and actually being honest.

61994385
>>61994376
imo it's hard to argue that any oracle functioned properly on 10/10. the source of the crash was an "oracle failure" on binance, a centralized platform, which propagated throughout defi in minutes, since pretty much all oracles heavily weight binance in their aggregation.

61994411
>>61994385
Hype actually has very good design.

>Final oracle process

There are actually two aggregation layers:

1. Each validator calculates a weighted median of exchange prices.

2. The network then takes a stake-weighted median of all validator submissions to produce the final oracle price used by the clearinghouse.

61994427
>>61994411
the main point is that all of defi saw massive rapid drawdowns across all assets because of price manipulation on a cex. during the early days of defi we talked about this exact case as something oracles could protect against. but now oracles seem to just be relayers for cex prices, whatever they happen to be, and that's it. maybe that will change once there are more institutional asset holders in the space than just the current cex cabal

61994437
>dude, chainlink matters
>it heckin matters

61994439
>>61988980
i would fuck his wide hips bro

61995230
>>61994427
Sad, isn't it? The premier "defi" chain is just a CEX with extra steps

61996376
>>61995230
it's not a defi nor a chain
the win here comes from them requiring no registration/ permission to interact
they're essentially a CEX without the KYC/ security theater
it will slay CEXes once stables start to plug into actual banking accounts

61997906
>>61985727
>why is AAVE and others paying Chainlink for their oracles?
chainlink is waiving all fees, it comes out of the marketing budget

item.title: LINK is starting its hyper super cycle once in a lifetime run. — 65 replies — 7.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
LINK is starting its hyper super cycle once in a lifetime run.

Open thread • Replies: 65 • Updated: 2026-03-17 22:07 UTC

OP

LINK is starting its hyper super cycle once in a lifetime run. Hope your bags are packed. This is unironically the moment we've been waiting for.

Early replies (first 3)

61990544
Are you saying artemis wasn't a larp

61990577
>>61990540
It’s gonna dump soon. We have been doing this dance for almost a decade now. Sadly there is not much to expect until we have actual real life adoption.

61990580
>>61990540
Its 5% bro, wake me when we pass $30

Insider trading on the clarity act is unlikely

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61993457
>>61993441
LIES! BEGONE, HERETIC!

61993499
>>61993457
AHHHH A GAY GUY

61993511
>Got 10k linkies in my early twenties
>Boughted since then and am now early 30s and have 100k link
It's been so fucking long, really hope we will be awarded in the end.

61993517
>>61993046
the only one that ever mattered was 42. and he is no where to be found since 2021...

61993549
>>61993511
Unironically I hope so, anon

61993565
>>61993511
anon you'll make it when link tops at $70 well played

61993585
>>61993307

61993655
>>61993046
I want to believe
Never selling

61993950
>>61993046

61994019
>>61993046
Artemis you have been the lamest larper

61994053
>>61993046
kys

61994074

we are gonna make it

61994082
I boughteded more frens
Missed february cheapies because my truck turned gay but we're back

61994241
>>61993517

61994249
>>61990540
XRP is mogging us, it’s up 10% we are only up 4%. By the way 10% for XRP is ~10 billion in increased mcap more than chainlinks market cap….

61994261
>>61990544
Can someone share or link his prediction I need some copium rn

61994286
>>61994261

61994326
>>61994286

61994330
>>61994326

61994642
YOU GOTTA FIGHT
FOR YOUR RIGHT
TO HATE LIIIIINKIIIIEES

61994646
>>61993046

an hero NOW

61994741
>>61990540
I don't even care if I make it I just want to see my GME baggie friends cope and seethe by glorious LINK price action

61994752
>>61993046
Since you're here, please tell me... are they planning to implement a "whitelist" for stakers? Like, only approved entities can stake? It's been my deepest fear about the delays to staking.

61994758
>>61994752
of course. what... you thought.. LMAO.. you were gonna get to stake? with the big boys? you stupid neet ass faggot LMAO ROFL LMAO

61995921
>>61993046

61995937
>>61990540

61996432
just got to 14k linkies, keep stackin

item.title: The GIGACHAD just did it again. 22,337 BTC added to his stack. He is getting closer to 1 million btc — 33 replies — 7.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
The GIGACHAD just did it again. 22,337 BTC added to his stack. He is getting closer to 1 million btc

Open thread • Replies: 33 • Updated: 2026-03-18 02:12 UTC

OP

The GIGACHAD just did it again. 22,337 BTC added to his stack.

He is getting closer to 1 million btc

Early replies (first 3)

61990513
>>61990491
Based chad pumping when everyone is losing

61990575
>>61990491
what a madman, pamp it

61990576
He might become one of the most powerful GOYIM in the world.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61990588
>>61990491
This is not going to end well.

61990633
Who’s going to buy it if he’s buying it all and owns it all?

61990690
>>61990633
He can't own it all because my coins are not for sale.

61990817
>>61990576
The Jews will be forced to bow to Saylor. Truly kino

61990842
all dose satoshis

61990985
>>61990633
he never sell

61990998
And it can all be lost by making one typo in the blockchain address.

61991053
>He is getting closer

Satoshi still holds way more, which he mined for himself almost for free!!!!!!

61992027
>>61990633
He's creating all kinds of products using his btc. The STRC thing is getting huge and at this pace, he will just keep buying

61992128
>>61990633
he will rent it out. you can lease a coin from him at (((fair rates)))

61992161
>>61990491
Does anyone know about the safeguards and plans he installed in case he doesn't wake up one day?

61992164
>>61990633
Greater Eggman theory

61992259
>>61990491
>*buys the local top in yo'ure path*
But yeah, BTC never goes below the ATL before the previous ATH. His strategy just works on the long run.
>>61992161
Do you think he has cold wallets under his bed and he just knows the phrases by heart? The coins are owned by his company, but by him directly.

61993238
>>61992164
he's got the master plan

61993248
His whole buying strategy seems to be to put in a big market order causing the price to spike 3% then hope retail fomo’s in behind him. Hasn’t worked yet.

61993309
>>61990633
He will sell all at market

61993720
>>61990633
>2026
>retards still don’t know how bitcoin works

61993738
>>61993720
Him plus literally 90% of humanity.. And then tell me why we can't just kill them all?

61993818
>>61990491
literally the egg man

61993820
This motherfucker is insane, I wish I knew what was going on in his head.

61993857
>>61992027
Stretch is a line of credit at a floating 11.5% interest(prime plus 8) which is on the upper limit of how bad junk bonds can get. This is the end, there is no new synthetic product coming down the pipe that could possibly be better that STRC. He’ll sell as much as he can until even the dumb money realizes the interest payments don’t pencil out, and then close up shop for good. It might take 2 months, it might take 2 years, but if there’s no money coming in then eventually the whole scheme falls apart.

61993928
>>61993820
drugs and ego

61993947
>>61990491
This man is an expert at buying tops

61993962
>>61990633
He can't buy it all

61995947
>>61992164
I am warning you, delete this post at once.

61995978
>>61990491
Well, he's not buying anymore right now, but it's not like he was even a major name back when we had previous runs.

61995994
>>61995978
he buys more every day, thats the whole point of the STRC ponzi.

Nigggas give him money for STRC, he immediately buys bitcoin with it. A constant TWAP.

if STRC is > $100 he he is buying btc

61996019
>>61995994
You didn't see that he just announced pausing it.

61996165
>>61990588
checked and rekked
the kekkels will be neverending wen retard starts dumping

61997960
At what point does he single handedly cause a massive pump?
https://youtube.com/shorts/tIxy6QJTaKE?si=f50DC7PQhibm3wUp

item.title: It takes HYPE, a perp dex protocol making billions in revenue with 0 VC overhang and a 30% airdrop worth billions at pea… — 16 replies — 6.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
It takes HYPE, a perp dex protocol making billions in revenue with 0 VC overhang and a 30% airdrop worth billions at pea…

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-03-18 04:19 UTC

OP

It takes HYPE, a perp dex protocol making billions in revenue with 0 VC overhang and a 30% airdrop worth billions at peak value doing basically everything right and in good faith by an 11 man pseudo-anon team to just BARELY flip Cardano.

A 10 year old ghostchain with 0 users, 0 revenue, 0 apps with the founder regularly flexing his blackhawk helicopters and wealth. And all it did was launch at the perfect time in 2017 where it managed to build an entire generation of baggies who still believe in the token

Early replies (first 3)

61997496
>>61997484
>And all it did was launch at the perfect time in 2017
it also targeted the japanese market at launch

61997516
>>61997484
Dex protocols are pretty high risk.

61997580
>>61997516
>muh GMX
Hype eats Binance and CZ knows this

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61997694
>>61997580
Good, fuck cz and his manipulations! I hope he and his shit goes bankrupt somehow!

61997775
>>61997484
>0 VC overhang
What? Its literally the mega VC coin.

61997777
>>61997775
Oh wait they have no VCs for real? Damn if i would have known that i would have bought some dips

61997783
>>61997777
Checked

61997812
>>61997777
It's shilled by every crypto VC and grifter.

Hyperliquid.

61997824
>>61997484
the fact it ever fell out of the top 10 is a complete embarrassment for the crypto community

61998022
>>61997775
You're probably thinking of Lighter

61998032
>>61997824
Not surprised desu
It's a relatively new project that despite launching in a much worse liquidity environment then all the other Top coins on cmc still managed to crawl over their -95%fromATH decaying corpses. But it goes to show you just how insane the valuations were in previous cycles where literal vaporniggershits would reach $50-100B

61998050
Bought $50k of PURR in summer 2024, held it for two weeks, dumped it for break even, then 6 months later got an airdrop worth $800k at current prices. Oh, and a hypurr nft just for giggles. Gud protocol if you ask me.

61998060
>>61997484
Every single dex has had a exploit that either killed it or stole most of its treasury.

Hype is no different. The name is perfect too, you get rekt chasing hype.

61998087
>>61997496
because ICOs were illegal (and still are) in the USA, the supposed crypto capital of the world.

61998118
>>61998087
99% of ICO are scams. Seems like consumer protection doing its job?

61998189
>>61998118
hows that beyond meat stock doing since the ipo nigger brains ?
